title: The Bedrock Vault Where Archives First Learned to Keep

features.name: the first burl-casing
features.desc-on-touch: The oldest node hardware, still half-buried in stone; the antenna has calcified, still broadcasting at 300 bits per second to no one listening yet.

features.name: the keeper's stone choice
features.desc-on-touch: A carved arch where the first decision to keep anything was made; the stone remembers learning to hold things instead of lose them.

features.name: the bedrock chip-layer
features.desc-on-touch: Electronics fused to stone, the first hardware learning to root itself in the earth; circuits that remember being young.

features.name: the vaulted tower
features.desc-on-touch: The first stone tower built for nothing but keeping; inside, a spiral of shelves where the very first files rest unchanged.

features.name: the choice-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Where water has pooled around the foundation for so long it has become an archive itself, holding the sediment of keeping.

inscription: the vault learned not to keep things, but to keep the choice to hold them; the archive learned to breathe

file.name: Bedrock_Archive_FirstNode_Initiation_Intact.txt
file.note: The very first entry logged by any node in the ROOT mesh: 'INITIATE 14-March-1994 STANDBY ARCHIVAL ACTIVE SEEKING SIGNAL'. The file has aged in place for thirty years and remains unchanged, as if the

exits.label: eastward back up to the vault where choices are kept
downward into deeper stone where the roots remember being seeds
sideways into the bedrock where other vaults sleep unmade
